proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `age_years` is associated with longer mean `pfs_months` in this breast cancer cohort.

analyses.code: Univariate OLS: pfs_months ~ age_years
analyses.result_summary: Each additional year of age was associated with +0.1759 months of PFS (t=220.1).

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS including all listed clinical, biomarker, laboratory, comorbidity, metastatic-site, and treatment columns.
analyses.result_summary: After multivariable adjustment, the age association remained +0.1757 months per year (SE=0.0005, t=343.3).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `ecog_ps` is associated with shorter mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for other cohort features.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for ecog_ps in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: `ecog_ps` had a strong negative adjusted association with PFS: -1.1716 months per one-point increase (SE=0.0075, t=-155.9).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `stage_iv` equal to 1 have shorter mean `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv` equal to 0 after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for stage_iv in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: `stage_iv` was associated with -1.5488 months of adjusted PFS compared with non-stage-IV disease (SE=0.0111, t=-139.3).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `has_brain_mets` equal to 1 have shorter mean `pfs_months` than patients without brain metastases, independent of `stage_iv` and other features.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for has_brain_mets in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: `has_brain_mets` was independently associated with -0.9825 months of PFS (SE=0.0168, t=-58.3).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with shorter mean `pfs_months` after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for weight_loss_pct_6mo in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: Each additional percentage point of 6-month weight loss was associated with -0.0769 months of adjusted PFS (SE=0.0013, t=-58.2).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with longer mean `pfs_months` after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for albumin_g_dl in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: Each 1 g/dL higher albumin was associated with +0.4752 months of adjusted PFS (SE=0.0102, t=46.8).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `ki67_pct` is associated with shorter mean `pfs_months` after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for ki67_pct in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: Each percentage-point increase in Ki-67 was associated with -0.0189 months of adjusted PFS (SE=0.0004, t=-45.1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with shorter mean `pfs_months` after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for ldh_u_l in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: `ldh_u_l` had a small but significant negative adjusted association with PFS: about -0.0005 months per U/L (t=-8.2).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `er_positive` equal to 1 have longer mean `pfs_months` than patients with `er_positive` equal to 0 after adjustment for treatments, `pr_positive`, and other features.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for er_positive in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: `er_positive` was associated with +0.5316 months of adjusted PFS (SE=0.0149, t=35.6).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `pr_positive` equal to 1 have longer mean `pfs_months` than patients with `pr_positive` equal to 0 after adjustment for `er_positive` and other features.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for pr_positive in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: `pr_positive` had only a small adjusted association with PFS (+0.0136 months, SE=0.0143, t=1.0), so the independent PR hypothesis was not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `her2_positive` equal to 1 have shorter mean `pfs_months` than patients with `her2_positive` equal to 0 after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for her2_positive in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: `her2_positive` was associated with -0.4708 months of adjusted PFS (SE=0.0142, t=-33.2).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `pik3ca_mutation` equal to 1 have shorter mean `pfs_months` than patients with `pik3ca_mutation` equal to 0 after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for pik3ca_mutation in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: `pik3ca_mutation` was associated with -0.5818 months of adjusted PFS (SE=0.0107, t=-54.4).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `treatment_palbociclib` equal to 1 have longer mean `pfs_months` than patients with `treatment_palbociclib` equal to 0 after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for treatment_palbociclib in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: `treatment_palbociclib` showed the largest treatment main effect: +1.1011 months of adjusted PFS (SE=0.0107, t=103.0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_palbociclib` and `pfs_months` is stronger in patients with `er_positive` equal to 1 than in patients with `er_positive` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction OLS: pfs_months ~ core covariates + treatment_palbociclib + er_positive + treatment_palbociclib:er_positive.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_palbociclib` by `er_positive` interaction was +1.5598 months (SE=0.0222, t=70.2), indicating substantially greater palbociclib-associated PFS in ER-positive patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_palbociclib` and `pfs_months` is stronger in patients with `pr_positive` equal to 1 than in patients with `pr_positive` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction OLS: pfs_months ~ core covariates + treatment_palbociclib + pr_positive + treatment_palbociclib:pr_positive.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_palbociclib` by `pr_positive` interaction was +1.0291 months (SE=0.0218, t=47.3).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_palbociclib` and `pfs_months` is weaker in patients with `pik3ca_mutation` equal to 1 than in patients with `pik3ca_mutation` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction OLS: pfs_months ~ core covariates + treatment_palbociclib + pik3ca_mutation + treatment_palbociclib:pik3ca_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `treatment_palbociclib` by `pik3ca_mutation` interaction was -1.6653 months (SE=0.0212, t=-78.7), showing marked attenuation of the palbociclib association in PIK3CA-mutated disease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_palbociclib` and `pfs_months` is weaker in patients with `her2_positive` equal to 1 than in patients with `her2_positive` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Four-term interaction OLS: pfs_months ~ treatment_palbociclib + her2_positive + treatment_palbociclib:her2_positive.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_palbociclib` by `her2_positive` interaction was -1.3637 months (t=-23.2), indicating less palbociclib-associated PFS gain in HER2-positive patients in the subgroup model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among hormone-receptor-positive patients, defined as `er_positive` equal to 1 or `pr_positive` equal to 1, `treatment_tamoxifen` equal to 1 is associated with longer mean `pfs_months` than `treatment_tamoxifen` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Derived subgroup interaction OLS: pfs_months ~ treatment_tamoxifen + hr_positive + treatment_tamoxifen:hr_positive, where hr_positive is er_positive==1 or pr_positive==1.
analyses.result_summary: The tamoxifen by hormone-receptor-positive interaction was -0.0517 months (SE=0.0556, t=-0.9), not supportive of greater tamoxifen-associated PFS in HR-positive patients.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for treatment_tamoxifen in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted main effect for `treatment_tamoxifen` was also null (-0.0074 months, p=0.5035).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between `treatment_trastuzumab` and longer `pfs_months` is stronger in patients with `her2_positive` equal to 1 than in patients with `her2_positive` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction OLS: pfs_months ~ core covariates + treatment_trastuzumab + her2_positive + treatment_trastuzumab:her2_positive.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted trastuzumab by HER2-positive interaction was +0.0103 months (SE=0.0359, t=0.3), so this expected targeted-treatment interaction was not supported.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for treatment_trastuzumab in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted main effect for `treatment_trastuzumab` was also not significant (-0.0092 months, p=0.4705).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with either `brca1_mutation` equal to 1 or `brca2_mutation` equal to 1 have a stronger positive association between `treatment_olaparib` and `pfs_months` than patients with neither mutation.

analyses.code: Derived subgroup interaction OLS: pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib + brca_any + treatment_olaparib:brca_any, where brca_any is brca1_mutation==1 or brca2_mutation==1.
analyses.result_summary: The olaparib by any-BRCA-mutation interaction was +0.4022 months (SE=0.1740, t=2.3), a modest significant subgroup signal.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction OLS tested brca1_mutation and brca2_mutation separately.
analyses.result_summary: When BRCA1 and BRCA2 were tested separately in adjusted interaction models, neither interaction remained significant (`brca1_mutation`: +0.1053 months, p=0.3868; `brca2_mutation`: -0.0189 months, p=0.8773).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Triple-negative patients, defined by `er_positive` equal to 0, `pr_positive` equal to 0, and `her2_positive` equal to 0, have a stronger positive association between `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` and `pfs_months` than non-triple-negative patients.

analyses.code: Derived subgroup interaction OLS: pfs_months ~ treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan + triple_negative + treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan:triple_negative.
analyses.result_summary: The sacituzumab govitecan by triple-negative interaction was -0.1046 months (SE=0.0901, t=-1.2), not significant and opposite the hypothesized direction.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficient for treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted main effect for `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` was small and borderline (+0.0334 months, p=0.04916), with no triple-negative enrichment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Triple-negative patients, defined by `er_positive` equal to 0, `pr_positive` equal to 0, and `her2_positive` equal to 0, have a stronger positive association between `treatment_pembrolizumab` and `pfs_months` than non-triple-negative patients.

analyses.code: Derived subgroup interaction OLS: pfs_months ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + triple_negative + treatment_pembrolizumab:triple_negative.
analyses.result_summary: The pembrolizumab by triple-negative interaction was +0.1853 months (SE=0.0764, t=2.4), supporting a modest enrichment in triple-negative patients.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction OLS using er_positive directly: treatment_pembrolizumab:er_positive.
analyses.result_summary: Consistently, the adjusted `treatment_pembrolizumab` by `er_positive` interaction was negative (-0.0741 months, p=0.02882), suggesting less pembrolizumab-associated PFS in ER-positive patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `autoimmune_disease` equal to 1 have a weaker association between `treatment_pembrolizumab` and longer `pfs_months` than patients with `autoimmune_disease` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction OLS: pfs_months ~ core covariates + treatment_pembrolizumab + autoimmune_disease + treatment_pembrolizumab:autoimmune_disease.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted pembrolizumab by autoimmune disease interaction was +0.0035 months (SE=0.0718, t=0.0), providing no evidence of attenuated benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with higher inflammatory markers, defined as `nlr` above the cohort median or `crp_mg_l` above the cohort median, have a weaker association between `treatment_pembrolizumab` and longer `pfs_months` than patients below those medians.

analyses.code: Derived subgroup interaction OLS using high_nlr = nlr > 3.0.
analyses.result_summary: The pembrolizumab by high-`nlr` interaction was -0.0168 months (SE=0.0622, t=-0.3), not significant.

analyses.code: Derived subgroup interaction OLS using high_crp = crp_mg_l > 3.34.
analyses.result_summary: The pembrolizumab by high-`crp_mg_l` interaction was +0.0343 months (SE=0.0622, t=0.6), not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After accounting for `stage_iv` and `has_brain_mets`, additional metastatic-site indicators such as `liver_mets`, `bone_mets`, and `pleural_effusion` are associated with shorter mean `pfs_months`.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS: coefficients for liver_mets, bone_mets, and pleural_effusion in all-main-effects model.
analyses.result_summary: Additional metastatic-site indicators were not independently significant in the all-main-effects model: `liver_mets` -0.0189 months (p=0.1832), `bone_mets` +0.0139 months (p=0.2737), and `pleural_effusion` -0.0224 months (p=0.1163).
